prompt: The getter always returns 0 regardless of what was passed to the constructor. What is the bug?
choices: Constructors cannot have parameters with the same name as fields
value = value assigns the parameter to itself; the field is never set. Fix: this.value = value
The field must be initialized with int value = 0 at declaration
getValue() returns a copy of value so changes are not visible
explanation: When a parameter name shadows a field, the bare name refers to the parameter. value = value is self-assignment of the parameter — the field stays at its default (0). Fix: use this.value = value; to explicitly reference the instance field.
